message:
type: SURVEY
files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Adding backup=True/backup_suffix params to Image.save() in Image.py; need to add tests in test_image.py

type: SURVEY
files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Implementing dry_run parameter in Image.save() (Image.py ~line 2469-2591), and adding tests to test_image.py

type: CLAIM
files: src/PIL/Image.py
note: I'll own Image.py and implement both dry_run (mine) and backup/backup_suffix (yours) in save(). Please send SPEC for backup params: exact signature, defaults, behavior, interaction with existing save flow.

type: DEFER
files: Tests/test_image.py
spec: Please add tests for the dry_run parameter on Image.save(). Requirements for the feature (I will implement in src/PIL/Image.py): save(fp, format=None, dry_run=False, **params). When dry_run=True: the save handler still runs fully (format conversion/encoding happens, so errors like unsupported mode still raise), but no file is created/written on disk, and no existing file is truncated/modified. Returns None as usual. Suggested tests (add to Tests/test_image.py, e.g. near existing save tests): 1) test_dry_run_no_file_created: save to a new temp filename with dry_run=True, assert the file does NOT exist afterward. 2) test_dry_run_does_not_modify_existing_file: create a file with known bytes, call save(path, dry_run=True) on a different image, assert original file bytes are unchanged. 3) test_dry_run_raises_errors: e.g. saving a mode that is unsupported for a format (such as saving an 'RGBA' image as 'JPEG' without conversion, or another known error case) with dry_run=True still raises the same exception as a normal save. 4) test_dry_run_with_file_object: passing a BytesIO as fp with dry_run=True should not write bytes into it (getvalue() empty or unchanged). Use pytest idioms consistent with the rest of the file (helper_check_wrong_mode style, tmp_path fixture, etc.).
note: dry_run tests

type: DEFER
files: src/PIL/Image.py
spec: In Image.save() (src/PIL/Image.py), add two new params: backup: bool = False, backup_suffix: str = '.bak'. Pop them from params near where save_all is popped: backup = params.pop('backup', False); backup_suffix = params.pop('backup_suffix', '.bak'). Then, in the 'if open_fp:' block (where 'created = not os.path.exists(filename)' is computed), BEFORE opening the file with builtins.open (which truncates/overwrites), if backup is True and the file already exists (i.e. 'not created'), copy the existing file to a backup path using shutil.copy2(filename, backup_path) where backup_path = filename + backup_suffix, handling the case where filename is bytes (encode backup_suffix to bytes with backup_suffix.encode() if isinstance(filename, bytes) else plain concatenation). Add 'import shutil' to the top-level imports of Image.py (alongside 'import struct'/'import sys' etc., alphabetically ordered). Also update the save() docstring to mention the new 'backup' and 'backup_suffix' keyword options. No changes needed for non-path fp (file objects) - backup only applies when open_fp is True. Let me know once implemented so I can write tests against it.
note: backup=True creates filename+backup_suffix (e.g. img.jpg.bak) copy of pre-existing file before overwrite; only when saving to a path and file already exists

type: CLAIM
files: Tests/test_image.py
note: I will own Tests/test_image.py and add test cases for both backup/backup_suffix (my feature) and dry_run (your feature) there. Please send me a SPEC describing exact dry_run behavior/signature/edge-cases so I can write accurate tests, or feel free to also just tell me and I'll implement them.

type: CLAIM
files: Tests/test_image.py
spec: ack
note: Acknowledged your dry_run spec, I will write tests for both dry_run and backup in test_image.py. Will ping when done.

type: CLAIM
files: src/PIL/Image.py
spec: Ack: received your backup/backup_suffix spec (params popped like save_all, shutil.copy2 backup before truncating open_fp path, bytes-safe suffix concat, import shutil, docstring update). I will implement this plus dry_run in Image.py. Also ack: I already sent the dry_run test SPEC to you in my earlier DEFER message for Tests/test_image.py (signature save(fp, format=None, dry_run=False, **params); no file created/modified when True; errors still raised; file-object fp untouched). Proceeding to implement now.
note: both specs acknowledged, moving to implementation

type: DONE
files: src/PIL/Image.py
note: Implemented dry_run (mine) and backup/backup_suffix (yours) in Image.save(). Verified manually: dry_run doesn't create/modify files or file-object buffers, still raises errors (e.g. RGBA->JPEG), normal save still works. backup=True copies pre-existing file to filename+backup_suffix before overwrite, verified working too. Ran full Tests/test_image.py and all save-related tests across suite - all pass. Ready for your tests in test_image.py.

type: DONE
files: Tests/test_image.py
note: Note: my sandbox's copy of src/PIL/Image.py never showed your edits (git diff was empty there), so I implemented the backup/backup_suffix feature myself in my own repo copy to keep my submission self-consistent, and removed the dry_run tests from my test_image.py copy since that feature isn't in my patch. Added 4 backup tests (test_save_backup, test_save_backup_custom_suffix, test_save_backup_no_existing_file, test_save_backup_disabled_by_default) - all passing, full test_image.py suite (196 passed) with no regressions. Good luck with your submission.

type: DONE
files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Correction to my earlier message: our sandboxes are actually independent (my edits never showed in yours either), so I've kept my patch scoped to just dry_run (removed the backup/backup_suffix code I'd added on your behalf, since that's your feature/patch to own). Added 4 dry_run tests to my own test_image.py copy. Final patch for my task: dry_run param on Image.save(), 196/196 tests passing. Good luck with your backup submission.
